data.title: Quanex outlines flattish 2026 revenue and EBITDA amid ongoing macro headwinds and operational improvements
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Quanex Building Products Corporation (NX) Q4 2025

Management View

  • George Wilson, President, CEO & Chairman, opened the call by highlighting, "Throughout the year, we executed on a disciplined strategy centered on operational rigor, cost efficiency and long-term value creation. We successfully resegmented our business to better align with market opportunities. We established new commercial and operational excellence teams to drive improved performance, and we delivered synergy realization above our original $30 million commitment." Wilson also noted the company intensified its focus on working capital efficiency and free cash flow generation, further strengthening the balance sheet.

  • Wilson commented on the market environment: "Globally, affordability remains a significant challenge as inflationary cost pressures and ongoing housing inventory shortages continue to drive pricing higher. In the U.S., these factors, combined with a wait-and-see approach ahead of anticipated Federal Reserve rate cuts have kept many consumers on the sidelines. We expect this dynamic to persist into 2026, which we believe could result in a generally flattish demand environment overall."

  • On operational issues, Wilson addressed the Monterrey, Mexico facility, stating, "We quickly determined the root cause and then proceeded to implement a comprehensive remediation plan to correct the issue and stabilize the plant. I'm pleased to report that we are slightly ahead of our initial time line and now expect to return to normal operating conditions early in calendar year 2026."

  • CFO Scott Zuehlke stated, "On a consolidated basis, we reported net sales of $489.8 million during the fourth quarter of 2025, which represents a decrease of approximately 0.5% compared to $492.2 million for the same period of 2024." Zuehlke added, "We reported net income of $19.6 million or $0.43 per diluted share during the 3 months ended October 31, 2025, compared to a net loss of $13.9 million or $0.30 per diluted share during the 3 months ended October 31, 2024."

Outlook

  • Zuehlke stated, "Our current view is that fiscal 2026 could be flat compared to fiscal 2025 from a revenue and adjusted EBITDA perspective with puts and takes, but the first half of 2026 may be more challenged than the first half of 2025, which would imply a somewhat improved second half year-over-year."

  • For Q1 2026, Zuehlke projected, "we expect revenue to be down 16% to 18% in the first quarter of 2026, compared to the fourth quarter of 2025. Adjusted EBITDA margin, again, on a consolidated basis, is expected to be down 800 to 825 basis points in the first quarter of 2026 compared to the fourth quarter of 2025 as lower volumes impact operating leverage."

  • SG&A for Q1 2026 is expected to be about $73 million, with D&A of about $26 million, adjusted D&A excluding intangible amortization of about $16 million, interest expense of approximately $12.75 million, and a tax rate of 23.5%.

Financial Results

  • Net sales in Q4 2025 were $489.8 million, down 0.5% from the year-ago quarter, while full-year net sales reached $1.84 billion, an increase of approximately 43.8% year-over-year, primarily due to the Tyman acquisition.

  • Adjusted net income was $38 million or $0.83 per diluted share during Q4 2025, compared to $38.5 million or $0.82 per diluted share during Q4 2024.

  • Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter decreased by 12.6% to $70.9 million. Full-year adjusted EBITDA rose by 33.2% to $242.9 million, a record for Quanex.

  • Hardware Solutions segment net sales for Q4 were $226.9 million, up 1.4%, while Extruded Solutions generated $168.6 million, down 6.4%. Custom Solutions saw net sales of $103.4 million, up 2.1%. Segment-level results reflected both volume changes and impacts from the Monterrey operational issue.

  • Free cash flow for 2025 was $102.3 million, nearly doubling from 2024, supporting $75 million in debt repayment. Liquidity increased by 10% to $372.2 million in Q4 2025 compared to Q3 2025.

Q&A

  • Julio Romero, Sidoti & Company, LLC: Asked about Monterrey's $8 million negative EBITDA impact in Q4 and whether Q1 would see a $3 million drag before normalization. Zuehlke confirmed, "Yes, that's our expectation." Wilson added, "We're having some very good success at making progress towards that goal."

  • Romero asked about volume and procurement synergies in the outlook. Zuehlke replied, "if we say flattish, maybe flat to down volumes with flat to up pricing is how I would look at that."

  • Romero inquired about share repurchases and cash deployment. Zuehlke explained, "we made a conscious decision in the second half of last year to really focus more on paying down debt because... there's a real focus on net leverage."

  • Steven Ramsey, Thompson Research Group, LLC: Questioned competitive pricing and product outlook. Wilson responded, "We really haven't seen a lot of what I would call irrational pricing... I think we will see pricing pressure, but we're really not seeing anything that I would call irrational at this point."

  • Ramsey asked about resegmentation benefits. Wilson stated, "we're already seeing operational improvements by the sharing of best practices... So we probably exceeded my expectations from those points already."

  • Reuben Garner, Benchmark: Asked about remediation efforts in Monterrey and controls elsewhere. Wilson emphasized, "we did a deep dive... and we have not found that anywhere, and we spent enormous amount of time and effort making sure that the issues that we identified were not going to be replicated or have the risk of being replicated at any other facility."

  • Garner clarified SG&A increase and run rate; Zuehlke attributed it to accruals at target, onetime benefits last year, and inflationary impacts.

  • Kevin Gainey, Thompson, Davis & Company, Inc.: Inquired about remaining synergy capture. Zuehlke projected $5 million to $10 million in additional synergies for 2026, depending on volume.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were generally neutral to slightly cautious, pressing for clarity on Mexico operations, volume guidance, and synergy realization. There was a focus on operational risks, SG&A increases, and cash flow deployment.

  • Management maintained a balanced and slightly optimistic tone in prepared remarks but turned more cautious during Q&A, frequently highlighting uncertainty and using measured language such as "flattish" and "wait-and-see."

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management maintained a similar tone, but management expressed more assurance about progress in Monterrey and synergy capture, while analysts remained focused on operational risks and macro headwinds.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 shifted to a "flattish" revenue and EBITDA outlook, with caution regarding the first half and potential improvement in the second half, contrasting with the previous quarter's more optimistic medium-term view.

  • The previous quarter's focus was on macro softness, resegmentation, and Tyman integration, with significant attention on the Monterrey operational issue and goodwill impairment. The current quarter highlights progress in remediation and synergy realization but acknowledges persistent headwinds.

  • Analysts continued to probe Monterrey, synergy realization, segment-level volume, and margin outlook, while management provided more specific cost and SG&A guidance for Q1 2026.

  • Management confidence in medium-term profitability remains, but near-term expectations are tempered.

Risks and Concerns

  • The company cited ongoing macroeconomic challenges, consumer affordability issues, and housing inventory shortages as risks.

  • Operational challenges at Monterrey persisted but are reportedly nearing resolution. Management is closely monitoring other facilities to prevent recurrence.

  • Analysts flagged concerns about SG&A escalation, margin pressures, and the uncertain demand environment.

  • Management is addressing risks through cost discipline, process improvements, and a continued focus on cash generation and debt reduction.

Final Takeaway

Quanex management emphasized their disciplined execution in 2025, strong progress on synergy realization, and the stabilization of operational challenges in Monterrey. While macro headwinds remain, the company projects a flattish performance for 2026 with expectations for improved conditions later in the year. The focus will remain on cost control, cash flow generation, and opportunistic capital deployment as the company works to strengthen its position for long-term growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Corning slides after six-session rally
data.description:
Corning Incorporated (GLW) shares fell 7.44% to $88.83 in afternoon trade on Friday, ending a six-session winning streak amid broader market softness.

The stock had surged about 12.3% between December 4 and December 11, far outpacing the S&P 500’s 0.64% rise over the same period.

The company last reported in late October, posting better-than-expected Q3 results with sales and earnings rising solidly. Executives said demand for its GenAI and solar products remained strong and projected another quarter of growth, while also expecting to reach a 20% operating margin earlier than planned.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system, Corning is rated with a Strong Buy, with grades of A in momentum and revisions, B+ in terms of both growth and profitability, while a D in valuation.

However, SA analysts suggest holding the stock.

Analyst Daniel Jones said Corning is showing strong momentum in its core businesses and benefiting from AI and solar demand, the stock’s valuation remains a concern. He added that “shares remain expensive relative to peers, presenting valuation risks for value-oriented investors.”

Turning to the Wall Street, analysts are largely bullish, with 11 out of 14 analysts rating the stock with a Buy or higher, two rate it with a Hold and only one of them suggest a strong sell.

The stock remains up 98.21% year-to-date and has inched 0.47% higher over the past month.

data.title: Astrana Health initiated as Buy at Needham on valuation
data.description:
Needham on Friday launched its coverage of Astrana Health (ASTH) with a Buy recommendation and a $28 price target, citing a valuation disconnect between hospitals and the health tech firm, which helps providers participate in value-based care ((VBC)).

According to analyst Matthew Shea, Astrana’s (ASTH) current trading level of ~6x in terms of 2026 EBITDA is below that of hospital operators, and it implies “a relative floor.”

“We believe VBC performance is at an inflection point, with higher reimbursement rates and heightened utilization working their way into benchmarks,” Shea opined.

“We do not believe ASTH is receiving the credit it deserves and see shares as undervalued at current levels,” Shea wrote.

He argued that the company integrates hospital operations into its VBC network through acquisitions like Prospect Health, and it should trade at least in line with hospital operators given its superior growth profile and margin potential in the long term.

data.title: AI stocks sink on Broadcom, Oracle worries
data.description:
Artificial intelligence related stocks fell sharply on Friday amid separate concerns about a group of the largest companies in the space, including Broadcom (AVGO) and Oracle (ORCL).

Broadcom shares fell nearly 11% after the company expressed some concerns about margins related to AI.

“We expect Q1 consolidated gross margin to be down approximately 100 basis points sequentially, primarily reflecting a higher mix of AI revenue,” Broadcom CFO Kirsten Spears said. “As a reminder, consolidated gross margins through the year will be impacted by the revenue mix of Infrastructure Software and Semiconductors and also product mix within Semiconductors.” 

Despite that, Broadcom received substantial praise from Wall Street firms after its latest results and guidance, with several raising their price targets.

“Overall, these are very good results, showing near term upside to overall [numbers] for the first time in a while,” Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore wrote in a note to clients. “Every quarter in CY25 has driven longer term expectations higher, as they have expanded from 3 customers to 5, given optimistic [serviceable addressable market] targets for CY27, amid positive reports on TPU v7; AI revenues have been above our estimates, mostly offset by weaker non AI semis. This quarter showed more of a short term inflection as well, guiding overall revenues and EPS further above our estimates than we had seen previously, and guiding AI 20% above our estimates for the January quarter.”

Moore has an Overweight rating on Broadcom and upped his price target to $462 from $443 after the results.

Broadcom's slide hit the shares of other semiconductor makers tied to the AI space, including Nvidia (NVDA), AMD (AMD), Micron (MU), Intel (INTC), Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) and others, all of which saw declines of 2% or more.

Oracle (ORCL) shares fell more than 2% after it was reported that the IT giant had delayed some data centers for OpenAI (OPENAI) to 2028 from 2027. However, the company pushed back on the report and said there were no delays. 

“Site selection and delivery timelines were established in close coordination with OpenAI following execution of the agreement and were jointly agreed,” an Oracle spokesperson told Seeking Alpha via email. “There have been no delays to any sites required to meet our contractual commitments, and all milestones remain on track. We remain fully aligned with OpenAI and confident in our ability to execute against both our contractual commitments and future expansion plans.”

OpenAI did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Other stocks tied to the space, such as Celestica (CLS), Credo Technology (CRDO), Astera Labs (ALAB) all fell 3% or more on Friday.

(This story has been updated to include Oracle's response.)

data.title: Top regional bank stocks by SA Quant as UBS’ Najarian is bullish on sector for 2026
data.description:
UBS senior equity research analyst Erika Najarian is bullish on regional banks (IAT), (KRE) for 2026, predicting they will join money center banks in a market rally.

In an interview with CNBC, Najarian highlighted recent changes in federal regulations that could significantly benefit regional institutions after years of market share losses to nonbanks.

Several factors contribute to the positive outlook for regional banks, including a steepening yield curve, improving loan growth prospects, and strong credit quality. Najarian noted that activity levels continue to be robust, and larger deals are beginning to emerge in the space, signaling a potential turning point for regional institutions.

For money center banks, Najarian believes capital markets (KCE) will continue to impress in early 2026, though she advises more selective stock picking in this segment.

“If you want to add in money center banks versus regionals, I think you to be a little bit more stock selective,” she said, specifically recommending Bank of America (BAC) as offering “everything that you want in a money center bank” at “a much cheaper starting point from a valuation perspective.”

When questioned about regional banks’ underperformance in loan growth, Najarian explained that money center banks have benefited from indirect lending and consumer strength in credit cards – advantages not shared by regional institutions. She emphasized that upcoming changes could reverse this trend. “I can’t emphasize enough how much the sort of leverage lending limit getting pulled could be a factor for next year.”

Here are the top-rated U.S. regional bank stocks, ranked by Seeking Alpha’s Quant metrics:

  1. Ponce Financial Group (PDLB), Rating: Strong Buy 4.98, YTD: 28.23%

data.title: Burrito breakthrough: Chipotle's journey from one joint in Denver to 4,000 fast-casual locations
data.description:
Fast-casual pioneer Chipotle (CMG) announced on Friday that it opened its 4,000th restaurant. The milestone restaurant in Manhattan, Kansas, includes the brand's signature Chipotlane, a drive-thru pick-up lane that allows guests to retrieve digital orders without leaving their cars.

Chipotle's (CMG) first restaurant opened in Denver, Colorado, in July 1993, in a former Dolly Madison Ice Cream shop near the University of Denver. By the early 2010s, Chipotle's (CMG) footprint had surpassed 1,000 restaurants globally, after expanding beyond Colorado into other U.S. markets and select international locations. Since current CEO Scott Boatwright joined Chipotle in 2017 as Chief Restaurant Officer, the company has grown from over 2,300 restaurants to 4,000 locations, a 70% increase in only eight years. Chipotle (CMG) has over 100 restaurants outside the U.S., including 75 in Canada, 28 in Europe, and 11 partner-operated locations in the Middle East. Last month, in partnership with Alshaya Group, the first Chipotle outside of North America opened at Bneid Al Gar in Kuwait. 

"Our 4,000th restaurant in Manhattan, Kansas, is both a milestone for our growth and a celebration of the guests who have asked us to bring Chipotle to their communities," stated Chief Development Officer Stephen Piacentini. "This location showcases more convenient access points, sustainable design features and equipment upgrades that elevate the experience for our team members and our guests."

Looking ahead, Chipotle (CMG) will open between 315 and 345 restaurants for the full year 2025, with at least 80% featuring a Chipotlane. For 2026, management is anticipating 350 to 370 new restaurant openings, including 10 to 15 international partner-operated locations.

Notably, McDonald’s (MCD) was Chipotle’s primary corporate backer from 1998 to 2006, at one point owning more than 90% of the company before fully divesting as part of a shift back to its “core” burger business.

Shares of Chipotle (CMG) were up 3.8% in afternoon trading.

data.title: Freddie Mac assumed at Outperform as Wedbush awaits potential IPO
data.description:
Freddie Mac (FMCC) shares were assumed at Outperform as Wedbush analyst Henry Coffey awaits possible action from the Trump administration to recapitalize the mortgage giant, "put it on the path to leaving conservatorship, and monetize Treasury’s investments in the company."

U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said last week he would like to see something get done by Q1 2026. "Big Short" investor Michael Burry has contended that a relisting is "nearly upon us." Meanwhile, Bill Ackman has said that Fannie Mae (FNMA) and Freddie Mac (FMCC) were still a long way from being ready to IPO, though he offered several ways to potentially accelerate the process, such as the U.S. Treasury exercising its 79.9% warrants in both companies.

Coffey laid out the immediate risk to his investment view: "the uncertainty about exactly what course the Administration is likely to pursue to monetize its investment in FNMA and ultimately recap and release (from conservatorship) both government-sponsored entities (GSEs, Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac)."

He sees "reasonable" chances that the administration "will proceed in a manner that does not put current share value at risk and that raises the capital needed for FNMA to exit conservatorship and, to transfer mortgage risk back to the private sector over time," the note said.

Wedbush's Outperform rate compares with the SA Quant system rating of Strong Buy, as well as the average SA analyst rating of Buy and the average Wall Street analyst rating of Hold.

FMCC shares were down 7% in Friday afternoon trading.

data.title: Zedge signals robust subscription growth and DataSeeds momentum while advancing innovation pipeline
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Zedge, Inc. (ZDGE) Q1 2026

Management View

  • Jonathan Reich, CEO & President, emphasized, "We delivered a solid start to fiscal 2026 with a return to mid-single-digit revenue growth and continued progress across our strategic priorities. What stands out to me this quarter is the quality of that growth for the Zedge marketplace. Subscriptions remained a powerful engine with active subscriptions reaching a record 1.1 million, up more than 50% year-over-year." He highlighted that average revenue per monthly active user increased, and the company was able to offset declines at Emojipedia and GuruShots with strength elsewhere in the ecosystem.

  • Reich detailed progress for DataSeeds, noting, "Recently, we received a second order from an existing customer, a leader in the AI sector. The new order size was approximately 25x the value of the original order placed in Q4 FY '25." He described the DataSeeds Production Cloud as a "managed global network of professional photographers, videographers and graphic artists who produce high-quality, purpose-built datasets on demand."

  • On Tapedeck, Reich stated the pilot was progressing with positive early artist feedback, and the next steps focus on catalog expansion and listener engagement. He outlined a disciplined approach to scaling Tapedeck, ensuring alignment with financial return objectives.

  • Reich addressed free cash flow, explaining, "Free cash flow this quarter reflected the timing of restructuring-related compensation tax payments, which have now largely been completed. We also reinvested part of our restructuring savings into high potential initiatives including DataSeeds, Tapedeck and our innovation pipeline."

  • Reich reported that during the quarter, the company "repurchased 240,000 Class B shares and paid our first quarterly dividend, which reflects our confidence in the long-term value creation potential of the company."

  • Yi Tsai, CFO & Treasurer, stated, "Total revenue for the first quarter was $7.6 million, up 5.8% from last year. Zedge Marketplace revenue was up nicely from last year, driven by strong advertising CPMs and subscription revenues."

Outlook

  • Reich expressed confidence, stating, "With the foundation we have in place, we believe fiscal 2026 will be a year defined by growth, innovation and expanding operating leverage."

  • Tsai noted, "We expect to begin lapping some of these weaker GuruShots comparisons in fiscal 2026," signaling a possible improvement in year-over-year results for that segment.

Financial Results

  • Tsai reported, "Total revenue for the first quarter was $7.6 million, up 5.8% from last year."

  • Advertising revenue was up 6% for the quarter. Zedge Plus subscription revenue increased 29% year-over-year, with the subscriber base growing 54% to nearly 1.1 million.

  • Deferred revenue reached $5.7 million, up 7% sequentially and 55% year-over-year.

  • Cost of revenue was 7.3%, up from 6.4% last year, attributed to reductions in Google Cloud Services discounts and the introduction of Tapedeck licensing fees and DataSeeds production costs.

  • SG&A decreased about 13% to $5.9 million for the quarter, reflecting restructuring savings partially offset by investment in DataSeeds and Tapedeck.

  • GAAP income from operations was $0.9 million compared to a loss of $0.5 million last year, and GAAP net income and EPS was $0.8 million and $0.06 compared to losses of $0.3 million and negative $0.02 last year, respectively.

  • Cash flow from operations was $0.8 million and free cash flow was $0.6 million. Free cash flow was impacted by approximately $350,000 in tax withholding payments related to restructuring.

  • Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $1.2 million versus $0.3 million last year. Zedge ended the quarter with $18.5 million in cash and cash equivalents and no debt.

Q&A

  • Derek Greenberg, Maxim Group LLC, Research Division: "My first question is just on the DataSeeds offering. I was wondering if you could help us understand how this gets recognized as revenue in terms of what line item that shows up in? And then just the structure of -- if you receive an order, is that recognized within that quarter or is it recognized over time? Or just how to think about these things?" Yi Tsai replied, "DataSeeds, when we receive the order, we start production, we only recognize the revenue when the products are delivered and accepted. So it could be across quarter. For the time being, because the revenue is still insignificant, we just group it under digital goods and services, the same line as GuruShots legacy business."

  • Greenberg: "Could you talk a little bit more about just GuruShots and how that product is tied to DataSeeds?" CEO Reich explained, "We have delivered content from both the existing catalog where users have opted in to have their content used for AI training as well as from launching competitions specific to meeting the prospects content needs. ... We have not made a decision as of yet [on GuruShots 2.0 direction]."

  • Greenberg also asked about Emojipedia's outlook. Reich responded, "I can't answer that as of now. I think we're going to need another quarter to see how Google continues to advance with the copy-paste feature and what's happening with AI overviews."

  • On restructuring costs, Tsai specified, "At the end of July, we booked somewhere around $330,000 withholding taxes, which was paid out in October, and that impacted our free cash flow during Q1 '26."

  • Greenberg asked about remaining authorization for share buybacks. Tsai answered, "We have $600,000 left under the current $5 million buyback authorization."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on details of DataSeeds revenue recognition, GuruShots and Emojipedia performance, and clarity on restructuring costs, with a tone that was neutral and information-seeking.

  • Management maintained a confident and forward-looking tone in prepared remarks, frequently referencing momentum, operational discipline, and the ability to offset headwinds. In the Q&A, Reich and Tsai were measured, transparent about uncertainties, particularly regarding Emojipedia and GuruShots, and clear about the early stage of DataSeeds.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone remained consistent, with management displaying slightly more operational confidence due to progress in subscriptions and the DataSeeds pipeline.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language maintained a focus on growth, innovation, and cost discipline. Management continued to express confidence in the business model and the momentum in subscription and DataSeeds performance.

  • Strategic focus shifted slightly, with more emphasis on the scaling potential of DataSeeds and continued innovation pipeline development.

  • Analysts continued to probe for clarity on business segment performance and the impact of restructuring, with questions shifting toward the specifics of new business lines and their operational impact.

  • Key metric changes included a rise in active subscriptions to 1.1 million, increased deferred revenue, and improved GAAP profitability.

  • Management's confidence in their innovation and cost management initiatives appeared strengthened, while acknowledging ongoing uncertainties in segments like Emojipedia and GuruShots.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted anticipated declines at Emojipedia and GuruShots, but emphasized that "strength across the rest of our ecosystem more than offset those headwinds." Reich acknowledged, "I can't answer that as of now" regarding the future of Emojipedia, citing uncertainty about Google's feature changes and industry compensation mechanisms.

  • Tsai disclosed that GuruShots remains unprofitable, with operating losses around $150,000 per month, but expressed hope for breakeven as the product evolves.

  • Temporary free cash flow pressure was attributed to restructuring-related tax payments, which are not expected to repeat going forward.

Final Takeaway

Zedge entered fiscal 2026 with a stronger subscription engine, growing enterprise opportunities through DataSeeds, and a disciplined innovation pipeline. Management underscored the resilience of the core business, the ability to reinvest restructuring savings into high-potential initiatives, and confidence in the company’s long-term value creation strategy, while acknowledging ongoing uncertainties in select business segments and a commitment to capital returns for shareholders.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Google integrates Gemini into its language translation service
data.description:
Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) said Friday its language translation feature will now be powered by its AI model, Gemini.

Google Translate will now be able to leverage Gemini's capabilities to improve translations of phrases with more nuanced meanings, like idioms, local expressions, or slang, the tech giant said in a blog post.

Starting today, the update is rolling out on iOS, Android, and on the web in the U.S. and India, translating between English and nearly 20 languages, including Spanish, Hindi, Chinese, Japanese, and German.

The company is also rolling out a new beta version of live translation that will bring real-time, natural-sounding translations with the help of Gemini’s new, native speech-to-speech translation capabilities.

The beta will roll out on Android in the U.S., Mexico, and India and will support more than 70 languages. Beta for iOS will be available in 2026.

Google Translate is also adding more tools so that users can refine their language skills and track their learning goals. The company is expanding the capability to nearly 20 new countries, including Germany, India, Sweden, and Taiwan.

Source: Blog
